name: Mint
why_now: Mint is cooling by nature and perfect in summer heat. A few leaves change the temperature of a dish or a drink entirely.
how_to_choose: Strongly fragrant when a leaf is pressed
Spearmint or peppermint both work for this edition
buy_this_much: One bunch. It goes further than you think.
price_note: One of the least expensive things in this basket — usually $1–2 a bunch at any grocery store.

flavor_paths.name: Cucumber-mint
flavor_paths.description: Lime, salt, mint. The simplest summer side.

flavor_paths.name: Fruit and mint
flavor_paths.description: Peaches, cherries, or blackberries with a few leaves and honey.

flavor_paths.name: Spritz
flavor_paths.description: Slapped and dropped into the Hillcrest Spritz.

weekday_uses: Added to a salmon rice bowl
Stirred into sparkling water with lime
weekend_use: In the Hillcrest Spritz. Or over a peach and blackberry plate with honey.
storage: Keep in water at room temperature or wrapped loosely in a damp paper towel in the refrigerator. Use within five days.
one_thing_to_learn: Slapping a mint leaf between your palms before using it releases the oils without bruising the leaf. It is worth doing.
